title: vasconcellea microcarpa subsp. heterophylla
intro: Vasconcellea microcarpa subsp. heterophylla, also known as the "Andean berry", is a plant species belonging to the family Tovariaceae, native to the Andean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues, offering numerous health benefits due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Vasconcellea microcarpa subsp. heterophylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in a variety of ways.
